steps.title: Use the clerical-aspect clue
steps.content: The narrator says better colors and lighting might have made the rooms less heavy, but doing so could have harmed the rectory's clerical aspect. That shows the furnishings were chosen deliberately to preserve an appropriate clerical appearance.

steps.title: Reject misread room descriptions
steps.content: Choice A invents an intended comfort that the passage does not state. Choice C overfocuses on extravagance rather than appropriateness. Choice D says the narrator finds decorations good-looking but impractical, which the passage does not support. The answer is B.
